speaker: RICHARD
text: How now, how now? What say the citizens?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now, by the holy mother of our Lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The citizens are mum, say not a word.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Touched you the bastardy of Edward’s children?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I did; with his contract with Lady Lucy

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And his contract by deputy in France;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Th’ unsatiate greediness of his desire

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And his enforcement of the city wives;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: His tyranny for trifles; his own bastardy,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As being got, your father then in France,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And his resemblance being not like the Duke.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Withal, I did infer your lineaments,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Being the right idea of your father,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Both in your form and nobleness of mind;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Laid open all your victories in Scotland,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your discipline in war, wisdom in peace,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your bounty, virtue, fair humility;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Indeed, left nothing fitting for your purpose

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Untouched or slightly handled in discourse.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And when mine oratory drew toward end,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I bid them that did love their country’s good

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Cry “God save Richard, England’s royal king!”

speaker: RICHARD
text: And did they so?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: No. So God help me, they spake not a word

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But, like dumb statues or breathing stones,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Stared each on other and looked deadly pale;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which when I saw, I reprehended them

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And asked the Mayor what meant this willful silence.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: His answer was, the people were not used

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To be spoke to but by the Recorder.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Then he was urged to tell my tale again:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: “Thus saith the Duke. Thus hath the Duke inferred”—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But nothing spoke in warrant from himself.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: When he had done, some followers of mine own,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: At lower end of the hall, hurled up their caps,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And some ten voices cried “God save King Richard!”

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And thus I took the vantage of those few.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: “Thanks, gentle citizens and friends,” quoth I.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: “This general applause and cheerful shout

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Argues your wisdoms and your love to Richard”—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And even here brake off and came away.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What tongueless blocks were they! Would they not speak?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will not the Mayor then and his brethren come?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The Mayor is here at hand. Intend some fear;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be not you spoke with but by mighty suit.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And look you get a prayer book in your hand

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And stand between two churchmen, good my lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For on that ground I’ll make a holy descant.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And be not easily won to our requests.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Play the maid’s part: still answer “nay,” and take it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I go. An if you plead as well for them

speaker: RICHARD
text: As I can say “nay” to thee for myself,

speaker: RICHARD
text: No doubt we bring it to a happy issue.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Go, go, up to the leads. The Lord Mayor knocks.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Welcome, my lord. I dance attendance here.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I think the Duke will not be spoke withal.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now, Catesby, what says your lord to my request?

speaker: CATESBY
text: He doth entreat your Grace, my noble lord,

speaker: CATESBY
text: To visit him tomorrow or next day.

speaker: CATESBY
text: He is within, with two right reverend fathers,

speaker: CATESBY
text: Divinely bent to meditation,

speaker: CATESBY
text: And in no worldly suits would he be moved

speaker: CATESBY
text: To draw him from his holy exercise.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Return, good Catesby, to the gracious duke.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Tell him myself, the Mayor, and aldermen,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: In deep designs, in matter of great moment

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: No less importing than our general good,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Are come to have some conference with his Grace.

speaker: CATESBY
text: I’ll signify so much unto him straight.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Ah ha, my lord, this prince is not an Edward!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He is not lolling on a lewd love-bed,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But on his knees at meditation;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Not dallying with a brace of courtesans,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But meditating with two deep divines;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Not sleeping, to engross his idle body,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But praying, to enrich his watchful soul.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Happy were England would this virtuous prince

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Take on his Grace the sovereignty thereof.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But sure I fear we shall not win him to it.

speaker: MAYOR
text: Marry, God defend his Grace should say us nay.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I fear he will. Here Catesby comes again.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now, Catesby, what says his Grace?

speaker: CATESBY
text: He wonders to what end you have assembled

speaker: CATESBY
text: Such troops of citizens to come to him,

speaker: CATESBY
text: His Grace not being warned thereof before.

speaker: CATESBY
text: He fears, my lord, you mean no good to him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Sorry I am my noble cousin should

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Suspect me that I mean no good to him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By heaven, we come to him in perfect love,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And so once more return and tell his Grace.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: When holy and devout religious men

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Are at their beads, ’tis much to draw them thence,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: So sweet is zealous contemplation.

speaker: MAYOR
text: See where his Grace stands, ’tween two clergymen.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Two props of virtue for a Christian prince,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To stay him from the fall of vanity;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And, see, a book of prayer in his hand,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: True ornaments to know a holy man.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Famous Plantagenet, most gracious prince,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lend favorable ear to our requests,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And pardon us the interruption

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of thy devotion and right Christian zeal.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord, there needs no such apology.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do beseech your Grace to pardon me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who, earnest in the service of my God,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Deferred the visitation of my friends.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But, leaving this, what is your Grace’s pleasure?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Even that, I hope, which pleaseth God above

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And all good men of this ungoverned isle.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do suspect I have done some offense

speaker: RICHARD
text: That seems disgracious in the city’s eye,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that you come to reprehend my ignorance.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You have, my lord. Would it might please your Grace,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: On our entreaties, to amend your fault.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Else wherefore breathe I in a Christian land?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Know, then, it is your fault that you resign

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The supreme seat, the throne majestical,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The sceptered office of your ancestors,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your state of fortune, and your due of birth,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The lineal glory of your royal house,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To the corruption of a blemished stock,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Whiles in the mildness of your sleepy thoughts,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which here we waken to our country’s good,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The noble isle doth want her proper limbs—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Her face defaced with scars of infamy,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Her royal stock graft with ignoble plants,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And almost shouldered in the swallowing gulf

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of dark forgetfulness and deep oblivion;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which to recure, we heartily solicit

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your gracious self to take on you the charge

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And kingly government of this your land,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Not as Protector, steward, substitute,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or lowly factor for another’s gain,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But as successively, from blood to blood,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your right of birth, your empery, your own.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For this, consorted with the citizens,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your very worshipful and loving friends,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And by their vehement instigation,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: In this just cause come I to move your Grace.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot tell if to depart in silence

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or bitterly to speak in your reproof

speaker: RICHARD
text: Best fitteth my degree or your condition.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If not to answer, you might haply think

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tongue-tied ambition, not replying, yielded

speaker: RICHARD
text: To bear the golden yoke of sovereignty,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which fondly you would here impose on me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If to reprove you for this suit of yours,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So seasoned with your faithful love to me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then on the other side I checked my friends.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Therefore, to speak, and to avoid the first,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And then, in speaking, not to incur the last,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Definitively thus I answer you:

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your love deserves my thanks, but my desert

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unmeritable shuns your high request.

speaker: RICHARD
text: First, if all obstacles were cut away

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that my path were even to the crown

speaker: RICHARD
text: As the ripe revenue and due of birth,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Yet so much is my poverty of spirit,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So mighty and so many my defects,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I would rather hide me from my greatness,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Being a bark to brook no mighty sea,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than in my greatness covet to be hid

speaker: RICHARD
text: And in the vapor of my glory smothered.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But, God be thanked, there is no need of me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And much I need to help you, were there need.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The royal tree hath left us royal fruit,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which, mellowed by the stealing hours of time,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will well become the seat of majesty,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And make, no doubt, us happy by his reign.

speaker: RICHARD
text: On him I lay that you would lay on me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The right and fortune of his happy stars,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which God defend that I should wring from him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord, this argues conscience in your Grace,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But the respects thereof are nice and trivial,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: All circumstances well considerèd.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You say that Edward is your brother’s son;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: So say we too, but not by Edward’s wife.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For first was he contract to Lady Lucy—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your mother lives a witness to his vow—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And afterward by substitute betrothed

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To Bona, sister to the King of France.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: These both put off, a poor petitioner,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A care-crazed mother to a many sons,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A beauty-waning and distressèd widow,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Even in the afternoon of her best days,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Made prize and purchase of his wanton eye,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Seduced the pitch and height of his degree

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To base declension and loathed bigamy.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By her in his unlawful bed he got

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This Edward, whom our manners call “the Prince.”

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: More bitterly could I expostulate,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Save that, for reverence to some alive,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I give a sparing limit to my tongue.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Then, good my lord, take to your royal self

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This proffered benefit of dignity,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If not to bless us and the land withal,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet to draw forth your noble ancestry

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: From the corruption of abusing times

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Unto a lineal, true-derivèd course.

speaker: MAYOR
text: Do, good my lord. Your citizens entreat you.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Refuse not, mighty lord, this proffered love.

speaker: CATESBY
text: O, make them joyful. Grant their lawful suit.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Alas, why would you heap this care on me?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I am unfit for state and majesty.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do beseech you, take it not amiss;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot, nor I will not, yield to you.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If you refuse it, as in love and zeal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Loath to depose the child, your brother’s son—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As well we know your tenderness of heart

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And gentle, kind, effeminate remorse,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which we have noted in you to your kindred

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And equally indeed to all estates—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet know, whe’er you accept our suit or no,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your brother’s son shall never reign our king,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But we will plant some other in the throne,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To the disgrace and downfall of your house.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And in this resolution here we leave you.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Come, citizens. Zounds, I’ll entreat no more.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, do not swear, my lord of Buckingham!

speaker: CATESBY
text: Call him again, sweet prince. Accept their suit.

speaker: CATESBY
text: If you deny them, all the land will rue it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will you enforce me to a world of cares?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Call them again. I am not made of stones,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But penetrable to your kind entreaties,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Albeit against my conscience and my soul.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cousin of Buckingham and sage, grave men,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Since you will buckle Fortune on my back,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To bear her burden, whe’er I will or no,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I must have patience to endure the load;

speaker: RICHARD
text: But if black scandal or foul-faced reproach

speaker: RICHARD
text: Attend the sequel of your imposition,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your mere enforcement shall acquittance me

speaker: RICHARD
text: From all the impure blots and stains thereof,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For God doth know, and you may partly see,

speaker: RICHARD
text: How far I am from the desire of this.

speaker: MAYOR
text: God bless your Grace! We see it and will say it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: In saying so, you shall but say the truth.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Then I salute you with this royal title:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Long live Richard, England’s worthy king!

speaker: ALL
text: Amen.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Tomorrow may it please you to be crowned?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even when you please, for you will have it so.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Tomorrow, then, we will attend your Grace,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And so most joyfully we take our leave.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come, let us to our holy work again.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Farewell, my cousin. Farewell, gentle friends.
